title: 200 Families Allege Land Loss for Angkor Resettlement
highlights: in 2005 and is now being  ...    More than 200 Siem Reap families say they are still owed compensation from the Apsara Authority for land being used to resettle displaced villagers from within the Angkor Archaeological Park. About 100 people representing 210 families gathered at the Apsara Authority office in Siem Reap on Tuesday to demand compensation they say they were offered back in 2005. At the time, the authority fenced off 160 hectares the families had been using for farmland, said protester Sam Mom.
